title: Bridge Essay: Perinatal stroke: mechanisms, management, and outcomes of early cerebrovascular

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Perinatal, stroke:, mechanisms,, management,, and.

**Perinatal stroke: mechanisms, management, and outcomes of early cerebrovascular brain injury.** — Lancet Child Adolesc Health. PMID: 30119760. DOI: 10.1016/S2352-4642(18)30173-1. Perinatal stroke encompasses aheterogeneous group of focal neurological injuries early in brain development that probably affects more than 5 million people worldwide. Many such injuries are symptomatic in the first days of life, including neonatal arterial ischaemic stroke, cerebral sinovenous thrombosis, and neonatal haemorrhagic stroke. The remaining focal neurological injuries usually present later in the first year with motor asymmetry, such as arterial presumed perinatal ischaemic stroke,
